18	class Sampler(nn.Module):
19	
20	    def __init__(self):
21	        super().__init__()
22	        self.topk_topp_sampler = TopKTopPSampler()
23	        self.pin_memory = is_pin_memory_available()
24	
25	    def forward(
26	        self,
27	        logits: torch.Tensor,
28	        sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
29	    ) -> SamplerOutput:
30	        # NOTE(woosuk): Use the original logits (before any penalties or
31	        # temperature scaling) for the top-k logprobs.
32	        # This is different from the V0 sampler, which uses the logits that
33	        # is used for sampling (after penalties and temperature scaling).
34	        # TODO(rob): provide option for logprobs post sampling.
35	        # See https://vllm-dev.slack.com/archives/C07UUL8E61Z/p1735907856007919 # noqa: E501
36	        num_logprobs = sampling_metadata.max_num_logprobs
37	        if num_logprobs is not None:
38	            raw_logprobs = self.compute_logprobs(logits)
39	
40	        # Use float32 for the logits.
41	        logits = logits.to(torch.float32)
42	        # Apply allowed token ids.
43	        logits = self.apply_allowed_token_ids(logits, sampling_metadata)
44	        # Apply bad words exclusion.
45	        logits = self.apply_bad_words(logits, sampling_metadata)
46	
47	        # Apply logits processors which can impact greedy sampling
48	        for processor in (sampling_metadata.logitsprocs.non_argmax_invariant):
49	            logits = processor.apply(logits)
50	
51	        # Apply penalties (e.g., min_tokens, freq_penalties).
52	        logits = self.apply_penalties(logits, sampling_metadata)
53	        # Sample the next token.
54	        sampled = self.sample(logits, sampling_metadata)
55	        # Convert sampled token ids to int64 (long) type to ensure compatibility
56	        # with subsequent operations that may use these values as indices.
57	        # This conversion is necessary because FlashInfer sampling operations
58	        # return int32 (while PyTorch argmax and topk return int64).
59	        sampled = sampled.long()
60	
61	        # Gather the logprobs of the topk and sampled token (if requested).
62	        # Get logprobs and rank tensors (if requested)
63	        logprobs_tensors = None if num_logprobs is None else \
64	            self.gather_logprobs(raw_logprobs, num_logprobs, token_ids=sampled)
65	
66	        # Use int32 to reduce the tensor size.
67	        sampled = sampled.to(torch.int32)
68	
69	        # These are GPU tensors.
70	        sampler_output = SamplerOutput(
71	            # The sampled tokens are expanded to 2D tensor with shape
72	            # [num_requests, 1], where each row represents one generated
73	            # token per request.
74	            sampled_token_ids=sampled.unsqueeze(-1),
75	            logprobs_tensors=logprobs_tensors,
76	        )
77	        return sampler_output
78	
79	    def apply_temperature(
80	        self,
81	        logits: torch.Tensor,
82	        temp: torch.Tensor,
83	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
84	        # Use in-place division to avoid creating a new tensor.
85	        return logits.div_(temp.unsqueeze(dim=1))
86	
87	    def greedy_sample(self, logits: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
88	        return logits.argmax(dim=-1).view(-1)
89	
90	    def sample(
91	        self,
92	        logits: torch.Tensor,
93	        sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
94	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
95	        """Sample logits based on sampling metadata.
96	
97	        The various logits processing functions called in this method
98	        may update the logits tensor in-place.
99	        """
100	
101	        assert not (sampling_metadata.all_greedy
102	                    and sampling_metadata.all_random)
103	        if sampling_metadata.all_random:
104	            greedy_sampled = None
105	        else:
106	            greedy_sampled = self.greedy_sample(logits)
107	            if sampling_metadata.all_greedy:
108	                return greedy_sampled
109	
110	        assert sampling_metadata.temperature is not None
111	
112	        # Apply temperature.
113	        logits = self.apply_temperature(logits, sampling_metadata.temperature)
114	
115	        # Apply logits processors that only apply to random sampling
116	        # (argmax invariant)
117	        for processor in sampling_metadata.logitsprocs.argmax_invariant:
118	            logits = processor.apply(logits)
119	
120	        # Apply top_k and/or top_p.
121	        random_sampled = self.topk_topp_sampler(
122	            logits,
123	            sampling_metadata.generators,
124	            sampling_metadata.top_k,
125	            sampling_metadata.top_p,
126	        )
127	
128	        if greedy_sampled is None:
129	            return random_sampled
130	
131	        sampled = torch.where(
132	            sampling_metadata.temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS,
133	            greedy_sampled,
134	            random_sampled,
135	            out=greedy_sampled,  # Reuse tensor
136	        )
137	        return sampled
138	
139	    def compute_logprobs(self, logits: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
140	        return logits.log_softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
141	
142	    def gather_logprobs(
143	        self,
144	        logprobs: torch.Tensor,
145	        num_logprobs: int,
146	        token_ids: torch.Tensor,
147	    ) -> LogprobsTensors:
148	        """
149	        Gather logprobs for topk and sampled/prompt token.
150	
151	        Args:
152	          logprobs: (num tokens) x (vocab) tensor
153	          num_logprobs: minimum number of logprobs to
154	                        retain per token
155	          token_ids: prompt tokens (if prompt logprobs)
156	                     or sampled tokens (if sampled
157	                     logprobs); 1D token ID tensor
158	                     with (num tokens) elements
159	                     Must be int64.
160	
161	        Returns:
162	          Top-k int indices tensor, (num tokens) x (num_logprobs + 1)
163	          Top-k float logprobs tensor, (num tokens) x (num_logprobs + 1)
164	          Sampled token rank tensor, (num tokens)
165	        """
166	        assert token_ids.dtype == torch.int64
167	        # Find the topK values.
168	        topk_logprobs, topk_indices = torch.topk(logprobs,
169	                                                 num_logprobs,
170	                                                 dim=-1)
171	
172	        # Get with the logprob of the prompt or sampled token.
173	        token_ids = token_ids.unsqueeze(-1)
174	        token_logprobs = logprobs.gather(-1, token_ids)
175	
176	        # Compute the ranks of the actual token.
177	        token_ranks = (logprobs >= token_logprobs).sum(-1)
178	
179	        # Concatenate together with the topk.
180	        indices = torch.cat((token_ids, topk_indices), dim=1)
181	        logprobs = torch.cat((token_logprobs, topk_logprobs), dim=1)
182	
183	        # Use int32 to reduce the tensor size.
184	        indices = indices.to(torch.int32)
185	
186	        return LogprobsTensors(indices, logprobs, token_ranks)
187	
188	    def apply_penalties(
189	        self,
190	        logits: torch.Tensor,
191	        sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
192	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
193	        if not sampling_metadata.no_penalties:
194	            assert sampling_metadata.prompt_token_ids is not None
195	            logits = apply_all_penalties(
196	                logits,
197	                sampling_metadata.prompt_token_ids,
198	                sampling_metadata.presence_penalties,
199	                sampling_metadata.frequency_penalties,
200	                sampling_metadata.repetition_penalties,

4	from typing import Optional
5	
6	import torch
7	import torch.nn as nn
8	
9	from vllm import envs
10	from vllm.logger import init_logger
11	from vllm.platforms import current_platform
12	
13	logger = init_logger(__name__)
14	
15	try:
16	    import flashinfer.sampling
17	    is_flashinfer_available = True
18	except ImportError:
19	    is_flashinfer_available = False
20	
21	
22	class TopKTopPSampler(nn.Module):
23	    """
24	    Module that performs optional top-k and top-p filtering followed by
25	    weighted random sampling of logits.
26	
27	    Implementations may update the logits tensor in-place.
28	    """
29	
30	    def __init__(self):
31	        super().__init__()
32	        if current_platform.is_cuda():
33	            if is_flashinfer_available:
34	                flashinfer_version = flashinfer.__version__
35	                if flashinfer_version < "0.2.3":
36	                    logger.warning(
37	                        "FlashInfer version >= 0.2.3 required. "
38	                        "Falling back to default sampling implementation.")
39	                    self.forward = self.forward_native
40	                elif envs.VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER is not False:
41	                    # NOTE(woosuk): The V0 sampler doesn't use FlashInfer for
42	                    # sampling unless VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER=1 (i.e., by
43	                    # default it is unused). For backward compatibility, we set
44	                    # `VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER` as None by default and
45	                    # interpret it differently in V0 and V1 samplers: In V0,
46	                    # None means False, while in V1, None means True. This is
47	                    # why we use the condition
48	                    # `envs.VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER is not False` here.
49	                    logger.info("Using FlashInfer for top-p & top-k sampling.")
50	                    self.forward = self.forward_cuda
51	                else:
52	                    logger.warning(
53	                        "FlashInfer is available, but it is not enabled. "
54	                        "Falling back to the PyTorch-native implementation of "
55	                        "top-p & top-k sampling. For the best performance, "
56	                        "please set VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER=1.")
57	                    self.forward = self.forward_native
58	            else:
59	                logger.warning(
60	                    "FlashInfer is not available. Falling back to the PyTorch-"
61	                    "native implementation of top-p & top-k sampling. For the "
62	                    "best performance, please install FlashInfer.")
63	                self.forward = self.forward_native
64	        elif current_platform.is_tpu():
65	            self.forward = self.forward_tpu
66	        else:
67	            self.forward = self.forward_native
68	
69	    def forward_native(
70	        self,
71	        logits: torch.Tensor,
72	        generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
73	        k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
74	        p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
75	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
76	        """
77	        PyTorch-native implementation of top-k and top-p sampling.
78	
79	        The logits tensor may be updated in-place.
80	        """
81	        logits = apply_top_k_top_p(logits, k, p)
82	        probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
83	        return random_sample(probs, generators)
84	
85	    def forward_cuda(
86	        self,
87	        logits: torch.Tensor,
88	        generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
89	        k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
90	        p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
91	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
92	        """More optimized implementation for top-k and top-p sampling."""
93	        if k is None and p is None:
94	            # We prefer `random_sample` over `flashinfer_sample` when sorting is
95	            # not needed. This is because `random_sample` does not require
96	            # CPU-GPU synchronization while `flashinfer_sample` does.
97	            probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
98	            return random_sample(probs, generators)
99	        if generators:
100	            logger.warning("FlashInfer 0.2.3+ does not support "
101	                           "per-request generators. Falling back to "
102	                           "PyTorch-native implementation.")
103	            return self.forward_native(logits, generators, k, p)
104	        # flashinfer sampling functions expect contiguous logits.
105	        # In flex_attn/triton_attn fp32 inference, logits can be non-contiguous
106	        # because of slicing operation in logits_processor.
107	        return flashinfer_sample(logits.contiguous(), k, p, generators)
108	
109	    def forward_tpu(
110	        self,
111	        logits: torch.Tensor,
112	        generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
113	        k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
114	        p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
115	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
116	        logits = apply_top_k_top_p_tpu(logits, k, p)
117	        probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
118	        return random_sample(probs, generators)
119	
120	
121	def apply_top_k_top_p_tpu(
122	    logits: torch.Tensor,
123	    k: torch.Tensor,
124	    p: torch.Tensor,
125	) -> torch.Tensor:
126	    """
127	    Apply top-k and top-p optimized for TPU.
128	
129	    This algorithm avoids using torch.scatter which is extremely slow on TPU.
130	    This is achieved by finding a "cut-off" element in the original logit, and
131	    after thresholding the logit using this cut-off, the remaining elements
132	    shall constitute the top-p set.
133	
134	    Note: in the case of tie (i.e. multipple cut-off elements present in the
135	    logit), all tie elements are included in the top-p set. In other words,
136	    this function does not break ties. Instead, these tie tokens have equal
137	    chance of being chosen during final sampling, so we can consider the tie
138	    being broken then.
139	    """
140	    probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1)
141	    probs_sort, _ = probs.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)
142	
143	    if k is not None:
144	        top_k_count = probs_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)  # shape: (batch, )
145	        top_k_count = top_k_count.unsqueeze(dim=1)
146	        top_k_cutoff = probs_sort.gather(-1, top_k_count)
147	
148	        # Make sure the no top-k rows are no-op.
149	        no_top_k_mask = (k == logits.shape[1]).unsqueeze(dim=1)
150	        top_k_cutoff.masked_fill_(no_top_k_mask, -float("inf"))

168	def apply_top_k_top_p(
169	    logits: torch.Tensor,
170	    k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
171	    p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
172	) -> torch.Tensor:
173	    """Apply top-k and top-p masks to the logits.
174	
175	    If a top-p is used, this function will sort the logits tensor,
176	    which can be slow for large batches.
177	
178	    The logits tensor may be updated in-place.
179	    """
180	    if p is None:
181	        if k is None:
182	            return logits
183	
184	        # Avoid sorting vocab for top-k only case.
185	        return apply_top_k_only(logits, k)
186	
187	    logits_sort, logits_idx = logits.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)
188	
189	    if k is not None:
190	        # Apply top-k.
191	        top_k_mask = logits_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)  # shape: B
192	        # Get all the top_k values.
193	        top_k_mask = logits_sort.gather(1, top_k_mask.unsqueeze(dim=1))
194	        top_k_mask = logits_sort < top_k_mask
195	        logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
196	
197	    if p is not None:
198	        # Apply top-p.
199	        probs_sort = logits_sort.softmax(dim=-1)
200	        probs_sum = torch.cumsum(probs_sort, dim=-1, out=probs_sort)
201	        top_p_mask = probs_sum <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
202	        # at least one
203	        top_p_mask[:, -1] = False
204	        logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_p_mask, -float("inf"))
205	
206	    # Re-sort the probabilities.
207	    logits = logits_sort.scatter(dim=-1, index=logits_idx, src=logits_sort)
208	    return logits
209	
210	
211	def apply_top_k_only(
212	    logits: torch.Tensor,
213	    k: torch.Tensor,
214	) -> torch.Tensor:
215	    """
216	    Apply top-k mask to the logits.
217	
218	    This implementation doesn't involve sorting the entire vocab.
219	
220	    The logits tensor may be updated in-place.
221	    """
222	    no_top_k_mask = k == logits.shape[1]
223	    # Set non-top-k rows to 1 so that we can gather.
224	    k = k.masked_fill(no_top_k_mask, 1)
225	    max_top_k = k.max()
226	    # topk.values tensor has shape [batch_size, max_top_k].
227	    # Convert top k to 0-based index in range [0, max_top_k).
228	    k_index = k.sub_(1).unsqueeze(1)
229	    top_k_mask = logits.topk(max_top_k, dim=1).values.gather(1, k_index.long())
230	    # Handle non-topk rows.
231	    top_k_mask.masked_fill_(no_top_k_mask.unsqueeze(1), -float("inf"))
232	    logits.masked_fill_(logits < top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
233	    return logits
234	
235	
236	def random_sample(
237	    probs: torch.Tensor,
238	    generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
239	) -> torch.Tensor:
240	    """Randomly sample from the probabilities.
241	
242	    We use this function instead of torch.multinomial because torch.multinomial
243	    causes CPU-GPU synchronization.
244	    """
245	    q = torch.empty_like(probs)
246	    # NOTE(woosuk): To batch-process the requests without their own seeds,
247	    # which is the common case, we first assume that every request does
248	    # not have its own seed. Then, we overwrite the values for the requests
249	    # that have their own seeds.
250	    if len(generators) != probs.shape[0]:
251	        q.exponential_()
252	    if generators:
253	        # TODO(woosuk): This can be slow because we handle each request
254	        # one by one. Optimize this.
255	        for i, generator in generators.items():
256	            q[i].exponential_(generator=generator)
257	    return probs.div_(q).argmax(dim=-1).view(-1)
258	
259	
260	def flashinfer_sample(
261	    logits: torch.Tensor,
262	    k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
263	    p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
264	    generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
265	) -> torch.Tensor:
266	    """Sample from the logits using FlashInfer.
267	
268	    Statistically, this function is equivalent to the `random_sample` function.
269	    However, this function is faster because it avoids sorting the logits tensor
270	    via rejection sampling.
271	
272	    NOTE: The outputs of this function do not necessarily match the outputs of
273	    the `random_sample` function. It only guarantees that the outputs are
274	    statistically equivalent.
275	
276	    NOTE: This function includes CPU-GPU synchronization, while `random_sample`
277	    does not. Call this function at the end of the forward pass to minimize
278	    the synchronization overhead.
279	    """
280	    assert not (k is None and p is None)
281	    if k is None:
282	        # Top-p only.
283	        probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
284	        next_token_ids = flashinfer.sampling.top_p_sampling_from_probs(
285	            probs, p, deterministic=True)
286	    elif p is None:
287	        # Top-k only.
288	        probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
289	        next_token_ids = flashinfer.sampling.top_k_sampling_from_probs(
290	            probs, k, deterministic=True)
291	    else:
292	        # Both top-k and top-p.
293	        next_token_ids = flashinfer.sampling.top_k_top_p_sampling_from_logits(
294	            logits, k, p, deterministic=True)
295	
296	    return next_token_ids.view(-1)
297

351	def _apply_min_tokens_penalty(
352	    logits: torch.Tensor,
353	    sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
354	) -> torch.Tensor:
355	    """Apply min_tokens penalty which sets stop tokens to -inf if min_tokens
356	        have not been generated yet
357	    """
358	    # list of indices in logits that will be set to -inf
359	    logits_to_penalize: list[tuple[int, int]] = []
360	    logits_applied = 0
361	    for seq_group in sampling_metadata.seq_groups:
362	        seq_ids = seq_group.seq_ids
363	        sampling_params = seq_group.sampling_params
364	
365	        sample_indices = seq_group.sample_indices
366	        logits_applied += len(sample_indices) + len(
367	            seq_group.prompt_logprob_indices)
368	        if not seq_group.do_sample:
369	            continue
370	
371	        start_idx = sample_indices[0]
372	        min_tokens = sampling_params.min_tokens
373	        token_ids_to_penalize = sampling_params.all_stop_token_ids
374	        if min_tokens > 0 and token_ids_to_penalize:
375	            seqs_to_penalize: list[int] = []
376	            for j, seq_id in enumerate(seq_ids):
377	                seq_data = seq_group.seq_data[seq_id]
378	                if len(seq_data.output_token_ids_array) < min_tokens:
379	                    seqs_to_penalize.append(j)
380	
381	            if seqs_to_penalize:
382	                # convert to the index into logits
383	                seqs_to_penalize = [start_idx + j for j in seqs_to_penalize]
384	                # itertools.product pairs each seq index with every token id
385	                logits_to_penalize.extend(
386	                    itertools.product(seqs_to_penalize, token_ids_to_penalize))
387	
388	    if logits_to_penalize:
389	        # use zip and * to group indices along each dimension
390	        # eg. [ (1,2), (1,3), (5,6) ] -> ( (1,1,5), (2,3,6) )
391	        logits[tuple(zip(*logits_to_penalize))] = -float("inf")
392	
393	    # verifies that no rows in logits were missed unexpectedly
394	    assert logits_applied == logits.shape[0]
395	    return logits

## Summary: vLLM Sampling Parameters Pipeline

I've traced the complete flow of sampling parameters (`top_p`, `top_k`, etc.) through vLLM's sampling pipeline. Here's the comprehensive mapping:

### Key Intermediate Data Structures

1. **SamplingParams** (Primary Input)
   - File: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775331504439-f1r1sm/vllm/sampling_params.py`
   - Fields: `top_p` (float = 1.0), `top_k` (int = 0), `temperature`, `min_p`, penalties, etc.
   - This is the user-facing configuration class

2. **SequenceGroupMetadata** (Flow Point 1)
   - File: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775331504439-f1r1sm/vllm/sequence.py` (lines 990+)
   - Carries `sampling_params: Optional[SamplingParams]` from scheduler to model runner
   - Passed through a list: `seq_group_metadata_list`

3. **SequenceGroupToSample** (Flow Point 2 - V0 Pipeline)
   - File: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775331504439-f1r1sm/vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py` (lines 20-62)
   - Intermediate dataclass holding: `sampling_params: SamplingParams`, seq_ids, seq_data, generator
   - Created in `_prepare_seq_groups()` function

4. **SamplingMetadata** (Flow Point 3 - V0 Pipeline)
   - File: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775331504439-f1r1sm/vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py` (lines 97-196)
   - Batched metadata containing list of `SequenceGroupToSample` objects
   - Prepared via static method: `SamplingMetadata.prepare()` (lines 148-188)

5. **SamplingTensors** (Flow Point 4 - V0 Pipeline - Where Values Get Tensored)
   - File: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775331504439-f1r1sm/vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py` (lines 372-597)
   - **Critical class** that converts SamplingParams to GPU tensors
   - Fields: `top_ps: torch.Tensor`, `top_ks: torch.Tensor`, `temperatures: torch.Tensor`, penalties tensors
   - Created via: `SamplingTensors.from_sampling_metadata()` method
   - **Key extraction logic** (lines 387-495):
     ```python
     for seq_group in sampling_metadata.seq_groups:
         sampling_params = seq_group.sampling_params
         top_p = sampling_params.top_p
         top_k = min(sampling_params.top_k, vocab_size)
         # ... tensors built from these values
     ```

### V1 Pipeline (Newer Implementation)

6. **SamplingMetadata (V1)** - Different structure
   - File: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775331504439-f1r1sm/vllm/v1/sample/metadata.py` (lines 12-43)
   - Direct GPU tensors: `top_p: Optional[torch.Tensor]`, `top_k: Optional[torch.Tensor]`
   - Built in GPU input batch

7. **GPUInputBatch (V1)** - Builds metadata from SamplingParams
   - File: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775331504439-f1r1sm/vllm/v1/worker/gpu_input_batch.py`
   - CPU buffers: `top_p_cpu`, `top_k_cpu` arrays
   - Extraction logic (lines 312-320):
     ```python
     self.top_p_cpu[req_index] = sampling_params.top_p
     top_k = sampling_params.top_k
     if 0 < top_k < self.vocab_size:
         self.top_k_reqs.add(req_id)
     self.top_k_cpu[req_index] = top_k
     ```
   - Creates SamplingMetadata via `_make_sampling_metadata()` (lines 600-656)

### Where Actual Filtering Happens

8. **Sampler.forward()** - V0 Pipeline
   - File: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775331504439-f1r1sm/vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py` (lines 225-334)
   - Line 216-223: Initializes SamplingTensors from SamplingMetadata
   - Line 285-286: Applies filtering:
     ```python
     if do_top_p_top_k and flashinfer_top_k_top_p_sampling is None:
         logits = _apply_top_k_top_p(logits, sampling_tensors.top_ps,
                                     sampling_tensors.top_ks)

## Summary: Top-P and Top-K Sampling Implementation in vLLM

I've analyzed the vLLM codebase and found comprehensive implementations of top_p and top_k sampling across multiple versions. Here's what I discovered:

---

### **File Locations**

**Main Implementation Files:**
1. `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775331504439-f1r1sm/vllm/v1/sample/ops/topk_topp_sampler.py` - V1 sampler with three implementations
2. `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775331504439-f1r1sm/vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py` - V0 sampler (legacy)
3. `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775331504439-f1r1sm/vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py` - Metadata/tensor preparation
4. `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775331504439-f1r1sm/vllm/v1/sample/metadata.py` - V1 metadata

---

### **1. V1 Implementation (Recommended)**
**File:** `vllm/v1/sample/ops/topk_topp_sampler.py`

#### **Class: TopKTopPSampler (Lines 22-119)**
Main module with three implementations based on platform:

```python
class TopKTopPSampler(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self):
        # Selects implementation: CUDA (FlashInfer if available), TPU, or native PyTorch
    
    def forward_native(logits, generators, k, p) -> torch.Tensor
        # PyTorch-native implementation
    
    def forward_cuda(logits, generators, k, p) -> torch.Tensor
        # FlashInfer-optimized for CUDA
    
    def forward_tpu(logits, generators, k, p) -> torch.Tensor
        # TPU-optimized implementation
```

#### **Function: apply_top_k_top_p (Lines 168-208)**
PyTorch-native CPU/GPU implementation:
```python
def apply_top_k_top_p(
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
    p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
) -> torch.Tensor:
    """Apply top-k and top-p masks to the logits.
    
    The logits tensor may be updated in-place.
    """
    if p is None:
        if k is None:
            return logits
        # Avoid sorting vocab for top-k only case.
        return apply_top_k_only(logits, k)

    logits_sort, logits_idx = logits.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)

    if k is not None:
        # Apply top-k: keep only top k values
        top_k_mask = logits_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)
        top_k_mask = logits_sort.gather(1, top_k_mask.unsqueeze(dim=1))
        top_k_mask = logits_sort < top_k_mask
        logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_k_mask, -float("inf"))

    if p is not None:
        # Apply top-p: keep tokens until cumulative prob >= (1-p)
        probs_sort = logits_sort.softmax(dim=-1)
        probs_sum = torch.cumsum(probs_sort, dim=-1, out=probs_sort)
        top_p_mask = probs_sum <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
        top_p_mask[:, -1] = False  # at least one
        logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_p_mask, -float("inf"))

    # Re-sort the probabilities back to original order
    logits = logits_sort.scatter(dim=-1, index=logits_idx, src=logits_sort)
    return logits
```

#### **Function: apply_top_k_only (Lines 211-233)**
Optimized top-k only (avoids full sorting):
```python
def apply_top_k_only(
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    k: torch.Tensor,
) -> torch.Tensor:
    """Apply top-k mask without sorting entire vocab."""
    no_top_k_mask = k == logits.shape[1]
    k = k.masked_fill(no_top_k_mask, 1)
    max_top_k = k.max()
    # Get the top-k threshold value
    k_index = k.sub_(1).unsqueeze(1)
    top_k_mask = logits.topk(max_top_k, dim=1).values.gather(1, k_index.long())
    top_k_mask.masked_fill_(no_top_k_mask.unsqueeze(1), -float("inf"))
    logits.masked_fill_(logits < top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
    return logits
```

#### **Function: apply_top_k_top_p_tpu (Lines 121-165)**
TPU-optimized implementation (avoids slow torch.scatter):
```python
def apply_top_k_top_p_tpu(
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    k: torch.Tensor,
    p: torch.Tensor,
) -> torch.Tensor:
    """Optimized for TPU - finds cutoff point instead of sorting."""
    probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1)
    probs_sort, _ = probs.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)

    if k is not None:
        top_k_count = probs_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)
        top_k_count = top_k_count.unsqueeze(dim=1)
        top_k_cutoff = probs_sort.gather(-1, top_k_count)
        
        no_top_k_mask = (k == logits.shape[1]).unsqueeze(dim=1)
        top_k_cutoff.masked_fill_(no_top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
        
        elements_to_discard = probs < top_k_cutoff
        logits.masked_fill_(elements_to_discard, -float("inf"))

    if p is not None:
        cumprob = torch.cumsum(probs_sort, dim=-1)
        top_p_mask = cumprob <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
        top_p_mask[:, -1] = False  # at least one
        
        top_p_count = top_p_mask.sum(dim=-1).unsqueeze(1)
        top_p_cutoff = probs_sort.gather(-1, top_p_count)
        elements_to_discard = probs < top_p_cutoff
        logits.masked_fill_(elements_to_discard, -float("inf"))

    return logits
```

#### **Function: random_sample (Lines 236-257)**
Performs weighted random sampling without torch.multinomial (avoids CPU-GPU sync):
```python
def random_sample(
    probs: torch.Tensor,
    generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
) -> torch.Tensor:
    """Randomly sample from probabilities using exponential trick."""
    q = torch.empty_like(probs)
    if len(generators) != probs.shape[0]:
        q.exponential_()
    if generators:
        for i, generator in generators.items():
            q[i].exponential_(generator=generator)
    return probs.div_(q).argmax(dim=-1).view(-1)
```

#### **Function: flashinfer_sample (Lines 260-296)**
Delegates to FlashInfer library for optimized CUDA sampling:
```python
def flashinfer_sample(
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
    p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
    generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
) -> torch.Tensor:
    """Use FlashInfer for faster rejection sampling."""
    if k is None:
        probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
        next_token_ids = flashinfer.sampling.top_p_sampling_from_probs(
            probs, p, deterministic=True)
    elif p is None:
        probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
        next_token_ids = flashinfer.sampling.top_k_sampling_from_probs(
            probs, k, deterministic=True)
    else:
        next_token_ids = flashinfer.sampling.top_k_top_p_sampling_from_logits(
            logits, k, p, deterministic=True)
    
    return next_token_ids.view(-1)
```

---

### **2. Min-P Implementation (Pattern Reference)**
**File:** `vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py` (Lines 427-441)

```python
def _apply_min_p(
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    min_p: torch.Tensor,
) -> torch.Tensor:
    """
    Adapted from text-generation-webui implementation.
    min_p: a probability threshold (typically 0.0-0.1)
    """
    probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
    top_probs, _ = probs.max(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
    scaled_min_p = min_p.unsqueeze_(dim=1) * top_probs
    tokens_to_remove = probs < scaled_min_p
    logits = logits.masked_fill_(tokens_to_remove, -float("inf"))
    
    return logits
```

**How it works:**
- Get max probability (best token)
- Scale min_p by max probability
- Remove tokens below `min_p * max_prob`

---

### **3. V0 Implementation (Legacy)**
**File:** `vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py` (Lines 398-424)

Same algorithm as V1's `apply_top_k_top_p` but called differently in orchestration.

---

### **4. Sampling Pipeline Orchestration**

#### **V1 Sampler Forward Method**
**File:** `vllm/v1/sample/sampler.py` (Lines 90-127)

```python
def sample(self, logits, sampling_metadata):
    # 1. Greedy sampling path
    if not sampling_metadata.all_random:
        greedy_sampled = self.greedy_sample(logits)  # argmax
        if sampling_metadata.all_greedy:
            return greedy_sampled

    # 2. Random sampling path
    logits = self.apply_temperature(logits, sampling_metadata.temperature)
    
    # 3. Apply logits processors (user-defined filters)
    for processor in sampling_metadata.logitsprocs.argmax_invariant:
        logits = processor.apply(logits)

    # 4. Apply top_k and/or top_p
    random_sampled = self.topk_topp_sampler(
        logits,
        sampling_metadata.generators,
        sampling_metadata.top_k,        # Tensor of shape (batch_size,)
        sampling_metadata.top_p,        # Tensor of shape (batch_size,)
    )

    # 5. Select between greedy and random
    return torch.where(
        sampling_metadata.temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS,
        greedy_sampled,
        random_sampled,
    )
```

#### **V0 Sampler Forward Method**
**File:** `vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py` (Lines 225-305)

```python
def forward(self, logits, sampling_metadata):
    # 1. Initialize sampling tensors (batched parameters)
    self._init_sampling_tensors(logits, sampling_metadata)
    sampling_tensors = self._sampling_tensors
    
    # 2. Apply penalties (repetition, frequency, presence)
    logits = apply_penalties(logits, sampling_tensors.prompt_tokens, ...)
    
    # 3. Apply temperature scaling
    logits.div_(sampling_tensors.temperatures.unsqueeze(dim=1))
    
    # 4. Apply top_k and top_p (if not using FlashInfer)
    if self._do_top_p_top_k and flashinfer_top_k_top_p_sampling is None:
        logits = _apply_top_k_top_p(
            logits,
            sampling_tensors.top_ps,     # Tensor of shape (batch_size,)
            sampling_tensors.top_ks,     # Tensor of shape (batch_size,)
        )
    
    # 5. Apply min_p (independent filtering)
    if self._do_min_p:
        logits = _apply_min_p(logits, sampling_tensors.min_ps)
    
    # 6. Convert to probabilities and log_probs
    probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1, dtype=torch.float)
    logprobs = torch.log_softmax(logits, dim=-1, dtype=torch.float)
    
    # 7. Sample tokens (multinomial or greedy)
    sample_results = _sample(probs, logprobs, sampling_metadata, ...)
```

---

### **5. Data Structures & Tensor Shapes**

#### **SamplingMetadata (V1)**
**File:** `vllm/v1/sample/metadata.py`

```python
@dataclass
class SamplingMetadata:
    temperature: Optional[torch.Tensor]      # Shape: (batch_size,)
    top_p: Optional[torch.Tensor]            # Shape: (batch_size,)
    top_k: Optional[torch.Tensor]            # Shape: (batch_size,)
    generators: dict[int, torch.Generator]   # Per-request RNG
    # ... other fields
```

#### **SamplingTensors (V0)**
**File:** `vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py` (Lines 373-495)

```python
@dataclass
class SamplingTensors:
    temperatures: torch.Tensor              # Shape: (num_tokens,)
    top_ps: torch.Tensor                    # Shape: (num_tokens,), dtype=float32
    top_ks: torch.Tensor                    # Shape: (num_tokens,), dtype=int
    min_ps: torch.Tensor                    # Shape: (num_tokens,), dtype=float32
    presence_penalties: torch.Tensor
    frequency_penalties: torch.Tensor
    repetition_penalties: torch.Tensor
    prompt_tokens: torch.Tensor
    output_tokens: torch.Tensor
```

**Creation Logic (Lines 391-495):**
- Per-sequence-group parameters are repeated for each token in that group
- Parameters are pinned to CPU memory initially, then moved to device as needed
- Disabled features are marked (e.g., `top_p=1.0`, `top_k=vocab_size`, `min_p=0.0`)

---

### **6. Key Algorithmic Details**

#### **Top-K Algorithm**
1. Sort logits in **ascending** order
2. Find the k-th largest value
3. Set all values below threshold to `-inf`
4. Soft-max to get valid probabilities

#### **Top-P Algorithm**
1. Sort logits in **ascending** order
2. Convert to probabilities
3. Compute cumulative sum of probabilities
4. Mark tokens where `cumsum <= 1 - p` to remove
5. Always keep at least 1 token (`top_p_mask[:, -1] = False`)
6. Set masked logits to `-inf`
7. Softmax to get valid probabilities

#### **Min-P Algorithm**
1. Compute probability distribution
2. Find max probability (argmax token)
3. Multiply `min_p * max_prob` to get threshold
4. Remove tokens with probability below threshold
5. Much simpler than top-p (no sorting needed)

---

### **7. Performance Optimizations**

| Optimization | Location | Benefit |
|--------------|----------|---------|
| Top-k only path | `apply_top_k_only()` | Avoids full sort when top-p disabled |
| TPU version | `apply_top_k_top_p_tpu()` | Avoids slow `scatter` operation |
| FlashInfer | `forward_cuda()`, `flashinfer_sample()` | CUDA-optimized C++ kernels |
| Exponential trick | `random_sample()` | Avoids `torch.multinomial` CPU-GPU sync |
| Pinned memory | `SamplingTensors.from_lists()` | Faster async CPU→GPU transfers |

---

### **8. Testing**
**File:** `tests/v1/sample/test_topk_topp_sampler.py`

Tests verify:
- Correctness with/without top-k only
- FlashInfer equivalence to PyTorch implementation
- Various batch sizes and vocab sizes

---

### **Key Takeaway**

The sampling pipeline follows this sequence:
```
Logits → Penalties → Temperature → Top-K/Top-P → Min-P → Softmax → Sample
```

Both top-k and top-p operate on **sorted logits** and use **masking** with `-inf` to disable unwanted tokens. Min-P is simpler (no sorting) and operates independently after top-p/k.

## Summary: Top-P and Top-K Sampling Implementation in vLLM

I've analyzed the vLLM codebase and found comprehensive implementations of top_p and top_k sampling across multiple versions. Here's what I discovered:

---

### **File Locations**

**Main Implementation Files:**
1. `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775331504439-f1r1sm/vllm/v1/sample/ops/topk_topp_sampler.py` - V1 sampler with three implementations
2. `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775331504439-f1r1sm/vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py` - V0 sampler (legacy)
3. `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775331504439-f1r1sm/vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py` - Metadata/tensor preparation
4. `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775331504439-f1r1sm/vllm/v1/sample/metadata.py` - V1 metadata

---

### **1. V1 Implementation (Recommended)**
**File:** `vllm/v1/sample/ops/topk_topp_sampler.py`

#### **Class: TopKTopPSampler (Lines 22-119)**
Main module with three implementations based on platform:

```python
class TopKTopPSampler(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self):
        # Selects implementation: CUDA (FlashInfer if available), TPU, or native PyTorch
    
    def forward_native(logits, generators, k, p) -> torch.Tensor
        # PyTorch-native implementation
    
    def forward_cuda(logits, generators, k, p) -> torch.Tensor
        # FlashInfer-optimized for CUDA
    
    def forward_tpu(logits, generators, k, p) -> torch.Tensor
        # TPU-optimized implementation
```

#### **Function: apply_top_k_top_p (Lines 168-208)**
PyTorch-native CPU/GPU implementation:
```python
def apply_top_k_top_p(
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
    p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
) -> torch.Tensor:
    """Apply top-k and top-p masks to the logits.
    
    The logits tensor may be updated in-place.
    """
    if p is None:
        if k is None:
            return logits
        # Avoid sorting vocab for top-k only case.
        return apply_top_k_only(logits, k)

    logits_sort, logits_idx = logits.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)

    if k is not None:
        # Apply top-k: keep only top k values
        top_k_mask = logits_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)
        top_k_mask = logits_sort.gather(1, top_k_mask.unsqueeze(dim=1))
        top_k_mask = logits_sort < top_k_mask
        logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_k_mask, -float("inf"))

    if p is not None:
        # Apply top-p: keep tokens until cumulative prob >= (1-p)
        probs_sort = logits_sort.softmax(dim=-1)
        probs_sum = torch.cumsum(probs_sort, dim=-1, out=probs_sort)
        top_p_mask = probs_sum <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
        top_p_mask[:, -1] = False  # at least one
        logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_p_mask, -float("inf"))

    # Re-sort the probabilities back to original order
    logits = logits_sort.scatter(dim=-1, index=logits_idx, src=logits_sort)
    return logits
```

#### **Function: apply_top_k_only (Lines 211-233)**
Optimized top-k only (avoids full sorting):
```python
def apply_top_k_only(
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    k: torch.Tensor,
) -> torch.Tensor:
    """Apply top-k mask without sorting entire vocab."""
    no_top_k_mask = k == logits.shape[1]
    k = k.masked_fill(no_top_k_mask, 1)
    max_top_k = k.max()
    # Get the top-k threshold value
    k_index = k.sub_(1).unsqueeze(1)
    top_k_mask = logits.topk(max_top_k, dim=1).values.gather(1, k_index.long())
    top_k_mask.masked_fill_(no_top_k_mask.unsqueeze(1), -float("inf"))
    logits.masked_fill_(logits < top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
    return logits
```

#### **Function: apply_top_k_top_p_tpu (Lines 121-165)**
TPU-optimized implementation (avoids slow torch.scatter):
```python
def apply_top_k_top_p_tpu(
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    k: torch.Tensor,
    p: torch.Tensor,
) -> torch.Tensor:
    """Optimized for TPU - finds cutoff point instead of sorting."""
    probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1)
    probs_sort, _ = probs.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)

    if k is not None:
        top_k_count = probs_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)
        top_k_count = top_k_count.unsqueeze(dim=1)
        top_k_cutoff = probs_sort.gather(-1, top_k_count)
        
        no_top_k_mask = (k == logits.shape[1]).unsqueeze(dim=1)
        top_k_cutoff.masked_fill_(no_top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
        
        elements_to_discard = probs < top_k_cutoff
        logits.masked_fill_(elements_to_discard, -float("inf"))

    if p is not None:
        cumprob = torch.cumsum(probs_sort, dim=-1)
        top_p_mask = cumprob <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
        top_p_mask[:, -1] = False  # at least one
        
        top_p_count = top_p_mask.sum(dim=-1).unsqueeze(1)
        top_p_cutoff = probs_sort.gather(-1, top_p_count)
        elements_to_discard = probs < top_p_cutoff
        logits.masked_fill_(elements_to_discard, -float("inf"))

    return logits
```

#### **Function: random_sample (Lines 236-257)**
Performs weighted random sampling without torch.multinomial (avoids CPU-GPU sync):
```python
def random_sample(
    probs: torch.Tensor,
    generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
) -> torch.Tensor:
    """Randomly sample from probabilities using exponential trick."""
    q = torch.empty_like(probs)
    if len(generators) != probs.shape[0]:
        q.exponential_()
    if generators:
        for i, generator in generators.items():
            q[i].exponential_(generator=generator)
    return probs.div_(q).argmax(dim=-1).view(-1)
```

#### **Function: flashinfer_sample (Lines 260-296)**
Delegates to FlashInfer library for optimized CUDA sampling:
```python
def flashinfer_sample(
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
    p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
    generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
) -> torch.Tensor:
    """Use FlashInfer for faster rejection sampling."""
    if k is None:
        probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
        next_token_ids = flashinfer.sampling.top_p_sampling_from_probs(
            probs, p, deterministic=True)
    elif p is None:
        probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
        next_token_ids = flashinfer.sampling.top_k_sampling_from_probs(
            probs, k, deterministic=True)
    else:
        next_token_ids = flashinfer.sampling.top_k_top_p_sampling_from_logits(
            logits, k, p, deterministic=True)
    
    return next_token_ids.view(-1)
```

---

### **2. Min-P Implementation (Pattern Reference)**

```

    # 1. Greedy sampling path
    if not sampling_metadata.all_random:
        greedy_sampled = self.greedy_sample(logits)  # argmax

    # 2. Random sampling path

    )

        sampling_metadata.temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS,

    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
        # how we deal with `best_of``:
        # if `best_of`` is not set, we default to `n`;
        # if `best_of`` is set, we set `n`` to `best_of`,
        # and set `_real_n`` to the original `n`.
        # when we return the result, we will check
        # if we need to return `n` or `_real_n` results
        if self.best_of:
            if self.best_of < self.n:
                raise ValueError(
                    f"best_of must be greater than or equal to n, "
                    f"got n={self.n} and best_of={self.best_of}.")
            if not self._real_n:
                self._real_n = self.n
                self.n = self.best_of

        if 0 < self.temperature < _MAX_TEMP:
            logger.warning(
                "temperature %s is less than %s, which may cause numerical "
                "errors nan or inf in tensors. We have maxed it out to %s.",
                self.temperature, _MAX_TEMP, _MAX_TEMP)
            self.temperature = max(self.temperature, _MAX_TEMP)

        if self.seed == -1:
            self.seed = None

        if self.stop is None:
            self.stop = []
        elif isinstance(self.stop, str):
            self.stop = [self.stop]

        if self.stop_token_ids is None:
            self.stop_token_ids = []

        if self.bad_words is None:
            self.bad_words = []

        if self.logprobs is True:
            self.logprobs = 1

        if self.prompt_logprobs is True:
            self.prompt_logprobs = 1

        # Number of characters to hold back for stop string evaluation
        # until sequence is finished.
        if self.stop and not self.include_stop_str_in_output:
            self.output_text_buffer_length = max(len(s) for s in self.stop) - 1

        self._verify_args()

        if self.temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS:
            # Zero temperature means greedy sampling.
            self.top_p = 1.0
            self.top_k = 0
            self.min_p = 0.0
            self._verify_greedy_sampling()

        # eos_token_id is added to this by the engine
        self._all_stop_token_ids.update(self.stop_token_ids)

    def _verify_args(self) -> None:
        if not isinstance(self.n, int):
            raise ValueError(f"n must be an int, but is of "
                             f"type {type(self.n)}")
        if self.n < 1:
            raise ValueError(f"n must be at least 1, got {self.n}.")
        if self.best_of is not None:
            if not isinstance(self.best_of, int):
                raise ValueError(
                    f"best_of must be an integer, got {type(self.best_of)}")
            if self.best_of < 1:
                raise ValueError(
                    f"best_of must be at least 1, got {self.best_of}")
            if self.best_of < self.n:
                raise ValueError(
                    f"best_of must be greater than or equal to n, "
                    f"got n={self.n} and best_of={self.best_of}.")
        if not -2.0 <= self.presence_penalty <= 2.0:
            raise ValueError("presence_penalty must be in [-2, 2], got "
                             f"{self.presence_penalty}.")
        if not -2.0 <= self.frequency_penalty <= 2.0:
            raise ValueError("frequency_penalty must be in [-2, 2], got "
                             f"{self.frequency_penalty}.")
        if self.repetition_penalty <= 0.0:
            raise ValueError(
                "repetition_penalty must be greater than zero, got "
                f"{self.repetition_penalty}.")
        if self.temperature < 0.0:
            raise ValueError(
                f"temperature must be non-negative, got {self.temperature}.")
        if not 0.0 < self.top_p <= 1.0:
            raise ValueError(f"top_p must be in (0, 1], got {self.top_p}.")
        # quietly accept -1 as disabled, but prefer 0
        if self.top_k < -1:
            raise ValueError(f"top_k must be 0 (disable), or at least 1, "
                             f"got {self.top_k}.")
        if not isinstance(self.top_k, int):
            raise TypeError(
                f"top_k must be an integer, got {type(self.top_k).__name__}")
        if not 0.0 <= self.min_p <= 1.0:
            raise ValueError("min_p must be in [0, 1], got "
                             f"{self.min_p}.")
        if self.max_tokens is not None and self.max_tokens < 1:
            raise ValueError(
                f"max_tokens must be at least 1, got {self.max_tokens}.")
        if self.min_tokens < 0:
            raise ValueError(f"min_tokens must be greater than or equal to 0, "
                             f"got {self.min_tokens}.")
        if self.max_tokens is not None and self.min_tokens > self.max_tokens:
            raise ValueError(
                f"min_tokens must be less than or equal to "
                f"max_tokens={self.max_tokens}, got {self.min_tokens}.")
        if self.logprobs is not None and self.logprobs < 0:
            raise ValueError(
                f"logprobs must be non-negative, got {self.logprobs}.")
        if self.prompt_logprobs is not None and self.prompt_logprobs < 0:
            raise ValueError(f"prompt_logprobs must be non-negative, got "
                             f"{self.prompt_logprobs}.")
        if (self.truncate_prompt_tokens is not None
                and self.truncate_prompt_tokens < 1):
            raise ValueError(f"truncate_prompt_tokens must be >= 1, "
                             f"got {self.truncate_prompt_tokens}")
        assert isinstance(self.stop_token_ids, list)
        if not all(isinstance(st_id, int) for st_id in self.stop_token_ids):
            raise ValueError(f"stop_token_ids must contain only integers, "
                             f"got {self.stop_token_ids}.")
        assert isinstance(self.stop, list)
        if any(not stop_str for stop_str in self.stop):
            raise ValueError("stop cannot contain an empty string.")
        if self.stop and not self.detokenize:
            raise ValueError(
                "stop strings are only supported when detokenize is True. "
                "Set detokenize=True to use stop.")
        if self.best_of != self._real_n and self.output_kind == (
                RequestOutputKind.DELTA):
            raise ValueError("best_of must equal n to use output_kind=DELTA")

    def _verify_greedy_sampling(self) -> None:
        if self.n > 1:
            raise ValueError("n must be 1 when using greedy sampling, "
                             f"got {self.n}.")

# SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
# SPDX-FileCopyrightText: Copyright contributors to the vLLM project

from dataclasses import dataclass
from typing import Optional

import torch

from vllm.v1.sample.logits_processor import LogitsProcessorManager


@dataclass
class SamplingMetadata:

    temperature: Optional[torch.Tensor]
    all_greedy: bool
    all_random: bool

    top_p: Optional[torch.Tensor]
    top_k: Optional[torch.Tensor]

    generators: dict[int, torch.Generator]

    # None means no logprobs, 0 means sampled token logprobs only
    max_num_logprobs: Optional[int]

    no_penalties: bool
    prompt_token_ids: Optional[torch.Tensor]
    frequency_penalties: torch.Tensor
    presence_penalties: torch.Tensor
    repetition_penalties: torch.Tensor

    output_token_ids: list[list[int]]

    # `allowed_token_ids_mask` is a 2D bool tensor of shape (max batch size,
    # vocab size).
    allowed_token_ids_mask: Optional[torch.Tensor]

    # req_index -> bad_words_token_ids
    bad_words_token_ids: dict[int, list[list[int]]]

    # Loaded logits processors
    logitsprocs: LogitsProcessorManager

                    req_id] = sampling_params.prompt_logprobs

            if sampling_params.allowed_token_ids:
                self.has_allowed_token_ids.add(req_id)
                if self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor is None:
                    # Lazy allocation for this tensor, which can be large.
                    # False means we don't fill with -inf.
                    self.allowed_token_ids_mask = torch.zeros(
                        self.max_num_reqs,
                        self.vocab_size,
                        dtype=torch.bool,
                        device=self.device)
                    self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor = torch.zeros(
                        self.max_num_reqs,
                        self.vocab_size,
                        dtype=torch.bool,
                        device="cpu")
                self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor[req_index] = True
                # False means we don't fill with -inf.
                self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor[req_index][
                    sampling_params.allowed_token_ids] = False

            if sampling_params.bad_words_token_ids:
                self.bad_words_token_ids[
                    req_index] = sampling_params.bad_words_token_ids
        else:
            assert request.pooling_params is not None
            self.pooling_params[req_id] = request.pooling_params

        # Add request lora ID
        if request.lora_request:
            lora_id = request.lora_request.lora_int_id
            if lora_id not in self.lora_id_to_request_ids:
                self.lora_id_to_request_ids[lora_id] = set()

            self.request_lora_mapping[req_index] = lora_id
            self.lora_id_to_request_ids[lora_id].add(request.req_id)
            self.lora_id_to_lora_request[lora_id] = request.lora_request
        else:
            # No LoRA
            self.request_lora_mapping[req_index] = 0

        return req_index

# SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
# SPDX-FileCopyrightText: Copyright contributors to the vLLM project
"""A layer that samples the next tokens from the model's outputs."""

import torch
import torch.nn as nn

from vllm.utils import is_pin_memory_available
from vllm.v1.outputs import LogprobsTensors, SamplerOutput
from vllm.v1.sample.metadata import SamplingMetadata
from vllm.v1.sample.ops.bad_words import apply_bad_words
from vllm.v1.sample.ops.penalties import apply_all_penalties
from vllm.v1.sample.ops.topk_topp_sampler import TopKTopPSampler

_SAMPLING_EPS = 1e-5


class Sampler(nn.Module):

    def __init__(self):
        super().__init__()
        self.topk_topp_sampler = TopKTopPSampler()
        self.pin_memory = is_pin_memory_available()

    def forward(
        self,
        logits: torch.Tensor,
        sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
    ) -> SamplerOutput:
        # NOTE(woosuk): Use the original logits (before any penalties or
        # temperature scaling) for the top-k logprobs.
        # This is different from the V0 sampler, which uses the logits that
        # is used for sampling (after penalties and temperature scaling).
        # TODO(rob): provide option for logprobs post sampling.
        # See https://vllm-dev.slack.com/archives/C07UUL8E61Z/p1735907856007919 # noqa: E501
        num_logprobs = sampling_metadata.max_num_logprobs
        if num_logprobs is not None:
            raw_logprobs = self.compute_logprobs(logits)

        # Use float32 for the logits.
        logits = logits.to(torch.float32)
        # Apply allowed token ids.
        logits = self.apply_allowed_token_ids(logits, sampling_metadata)
        # Apply bad words exclusion.
        logits = self.apply_bad_words(logits, sampling_metadata)

        # Apply logits processors which can impact greedy sampling
        for processor in (sampling_metadata.logitsprocs.non_argmax_invariant):
            logits = processor.apply(logits)

        # Apply penalties (e.g., min_tokens, freq_penalties).
        logits = self.apply_penalties(logits, sampling_metadata)
        # Sample the next token.
        sampled = self.sample(logits, sampling_metadata)
        # Convert sampled token ids to int64 (long) type to ensure compatibility
        # with subsequent operations that may use these values as indices.
        # This conversion is necessary because FlashInfer sampling operations
        # return int32 (while PyTorch argmax and topk return int64).
        sampled = sampled.long()

        # Gather the logprobs of the topk and sampled token (if requested).
        # Get logprobs and rank tensors (if requested)
        logprobs_tensors = None if num_logprobs is None else \
            self.gather_logprobs(raw_logprobs, num_logprobs, token_ids=sampled)

        # Use int32 to reduce the tensor size.
        sampled = sampled.to(torch.int32)

        # These are GPU tensors.
        sampler_output = SamplerOutput(
            # The sampled tokens are expanded to 2D tensor with shape
            # [num_requests, 1], where each row represents one generated
            # token per request.
            sampled_token_ids=sampled.unsqueeze(-1),
            logprobs_tensors=logprobs_tensors,
        )
        return sampler_output

    def apply_temperature(
        self,
        logits: torch.Tensor,
        temp: torch.Tensor,
    ) -> torch.Tensor:
        # Use in-place division to avoid creating a new tensor.
        return logits.div_(temp.unsqueeze(dim=1))

    def greedy_sample(self, logits: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        return logits.argmax(dim=-1).view(-1)

    def sample(
        self,
        logits: torch.Tensor,
        sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
    ) -> torch.Tensor:
        """Sample logits based on sampling metadata.

        The various logits processing functions called in this method
        may update the logits tensor in-place.
        """

        assert not (sampling_metadata.all_greedy
                    and sampling_metadata.all_random)
        if sampling_metadata.all_random:
            greedy_sampled = None
        else:
            greedy_sampled = self.greedy_sample(logits)
            if sampling_metadata.all_greedy:
                return greedy_sampled

        assert sampling_metadata.temperature is not None

        # Apply temperature.
        logits = self.apply_temperature(logits, sampling_metadata.temperature)

        # Apply logits processors that only apply to random sampling
        # (argmax invariant)
        for processor in sampling_metadata.logitsprocs.argmax_invariant:
            logits = processor.apply(logits)

        # Apply top_k and/or top_p.
        random_sampled = self.topk_topp_sampler(
            logits,
            sampling_metadata.generators,
            sampling_metadata.top_k,
            sampling_metadata.top_p,
        )

        if greedy_sampled is None:
            return random_sampled

        sampled = torch.where(
            sampling_metadata.temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS,
            greedy_sampled,
            random_sampled,
            out=greedy_sampled,  # Reuse tensor
        )
        return sampled

    def compute_logprobs(self, logits: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        return logits.log_softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)

    def gather_logprobs(
        self,
        logprobs: torch.Tensor,
        num_logprobs: int,
        token_ids: torch.Tensor,
    ) -> LogprobsTensors:
        """
        Gather logprobs for topk and sampled/prompt token.

        Args:
          logprobs: (num tokens) x (vocab) tensor
          num_logprobs: minimum number of logprobs to
                        retain per token
          token_ids: prompt tokens (if prompt logprobs)
                     or sampled tokens (if sampled
                     logprobs); 1D token ID tensor
                     with (num tokens) elements
                     Must be int64.

        Returns:
          Top-k int indices tensor, (num tokens) x (num_logprobs + 1)
          Top-k float logprobs tensor, (num tokens) x (num_logprobs + 1)
          Sampled token rank tensor, (num tokens)
        """
        assert token_ids.dtype == torch.int64
        # Find the topK values.
        topk_logprobs, topk_indices = torch.topk(logprobs,
                                                 num_logprobs,
                                                 dim=-1)

        # Get with the logprob of the prompt or sampled token.
        token_ids = token_ids.unsqueeze(-1)
        token_logprobs = logprobs.gather(-1, token_ids)

        # Compute the ranks of the actual token.
        token_ranks = (logprobs >= token_logprobs).sum(-1)

        # Concatenate together with the topk.
        indices = torch.cat((token_ids, topk_indices), dim=1)
        logprobs = torch.cat((token_logprobs, topk_logprobs), dim=1)

        # Use int32 to reduce the tensor size.
        indices = indices.to(torch.int32)

        return LogprobsTensors(indices, logprobs, token_ranks)

    def apply_penalties(
        self,
        logits: torch.Tensor,
        sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
    ) -> torch.Tensor:
        if not sampling_metadata.no_penalties:
            assert sampling_metadata.prompt_token_ids is not None
            logits = apply_all_penalties(
                logits,
                sampling_metadata.prompt_token_ids,
                sampling_metadata.presence_penalties,
                sampling_metadata.frequency_penalties,
                sampling_metadata.repetition_penalties,
                sampling_metadata.output_token_ids,
            )
        return logits

    def apply_allowed_token_ids(
        self,
        logits: torch.Tensor,
        sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
    ) -> torch.Tensor:
        if sampling_metadata.allowed_token_ids_mask is not None:
            logits.masked_fill_(sampling_metadata.allowed_token_ids_mask,
                                float("-inf"))
        return logits

    def apply_bad_words(
        self,
        logits: torch.Tensor,
        sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
    ) -> torch.Tensor:
        if sampling_metadata.bad_words_token_ids:
            apply_bad_words(
                logits,
                sampling_metadata.bad_words_token_ids,
                sampling_metadata.output_token_ids,
            )
        return logits

398	def _apply_top_k_top_p(
399	    logits: torch.Tensor,
400	    p: torch.Tensor,
401	    k: torch.Tensor,
402	) -> torch.Tensor:
403	    logits_sort, logits_idx = logits.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)
404	
405	    # Apply top-k.
406	    top_k_mask = logits_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)
407	    # Get all the top_k values.
408	    top_k_mask = logits_sort.gather(1, top_k_mask.unsqueeze(dim=1))
409	    top_k_mask = logits_sort < top_k_mask
410	    logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
411	
412	    # Apply top-p.
413	    probs_sort = logits_sort.softmax(dim=-1)
414	    probs_sum = probs_sort.cumsum(dim=-1)
415	    top_p_mask = probs_sum <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
416	    # at least one
417	    top_p_mask[:, -1] = False
418	    logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_p_mask, -float("inf"))
419	
420	    # Re-sort the probabilities.
421	    logits = torch.empty_like(logits_sort).scatter_(dim=-1,
422	                                                    index=logits_idx,
423	                                                    src=logits_sort)
424	    return logits
425	
426	
427	def _apply_min_p(
428	    logits: torch.Tensor,
429	    min_p: torch.Tensor,
430	) -> torch.Tensor:
431	    """
432	    Adapted from
433	    https://github.com/oobabooga/text-generation-webui/blob/3146124ec01f02c8fb1650a6517cf1b60b537aaf/modules/sampler_hijack.py#L16C17-L16C17
434	    """
435	    probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
436	    top_probs, _ = probs.max(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
437	    scaled_min_p = min_p.unsqueeze_(dim=1) * top_probs
438	    tokens_to_remove = probs < scaled_min_p
439	    logits = logits.masked_fill_(tokens_to_remove, -float("inf"))
440	
441	    return logits
442	
443	
444	def _greedy_sample(
445	    selected_seq_groups: list[SequenceGroupToSample],
446	    samples: torch.Tensor,
447	) -> SampleResultType:
448	    """Run greedy sampling on a given samples.
449	
450	    Args:
451	        selected_seq_groups: A list of sequence groups batched.
452	        samples: (num_selected_samples,) A tensor of samples. The length of
453	            samples could be smaller than selected_seq_groups if
454	            seq_group.do_sample is False.
455	    Returns:
456	        Tuple of (next_token_ids, parent_ids). The length of returned list is
457	        same as the length of selected_seq_groups. If the corresponding
458	        seq_group has do_sample=False, tuple contains ([], [])
459	    """
460	    samples_lst = samples.tolist()
461	    sample_idx = 0
462	    results: SampleResultType = []
463	    for seq_group in selected_seq_groups:
464	        if not seq_group.do_sample:
465	            results.append(([], []))
466	            continue
467	
468	        seq_ids = seq_group.seq_ids
469	        num_parent_seqs = len(seq_ids)
470	        assert num_parent_seqs == 1, (
471	            "Greedy sampling should have only one seq.")
472	        parent_ids = list(range(num_parent_seqs))
473	        next_token_ids = [samples_lst[sample_idx]]
474	        results.append((next_token_ids, parent_ids))

return logits


def _apply_top_k_top_p(
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    p: torch.Tensor,
    k: torch.Tensor,
) -> torch.Tensor:
    logits_sort, logits_idx = logits.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)

    # Apply top-k.
    top_k_mask = logits_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)
    # Get all the top_k values.
    top_k_mask = logits_sort.gather(1, top_k_mask.unsqueeze(dim=1))
    top_k_mask = logits_sort < top_k_mask
    logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_k_mask, -float("inf"))

    # Apply top-p.
    probs_sort = logits_sort.softmax(dim=-1)
    probs_sum = probs_sort.cumsum(dim=-1)
    top_p_mask = probs_sum <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
    # at least one
    top_p_mask[:, -1] = False
    logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_p_mask, -float("inf"))

    # Re-sort the probabilities.
    logits = torch.empty_like(logits_sort).scatter_(dim=-1,
                                                    index=logits_idx,
                                                    src=logits_sort)
    return logits


def _apply_min_p(
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    min_p: torch.Tensor,
) -> torch.Tensor:
    """
    Adapted from
    https://github.com/oobabooga/text-generation-webui/blob/3146124ec01f02c8fb1650a6517cf1b60b537aaf/modules/sampler_hijack.py#L16C17-L16C17
    """
    probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
    top_probs, _ = probs.max(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
    scaled_min_p = min_p.unsqueeze_(dim=1) * top_probs
    tokens_to_remove = probs < scaled_min_p
    logits = logits.masked_fill_(tokens_to_remove, -float("inf"))

    return logits


def _greedy_sample(
    selected_seq_groups: list[SequenceGroupToSample],
    samples: torch.Tensor,
) -> SampleResultType:
    """Run greedy sampling on a given samples.

    Args:
        selected_seq_groups: A list of sequence groups batched.
        samples: (num_selected_samples,) A tensor of samples. The length of
            samples could be smaller than selected_seq_groups if
            seq_group.do_sample is False.
    Returns:
        Tuple of (next_token_ids, parent_ids). The length of returned list is
        same as the length of selected_seq_groups. If the corresponding
        seq_group has do_sample=False, tuple contains ([], [])
    """
    samples_lst = samples.tolist()
    sample_idx = 0
    results: SampleResultType = []
    for seq_group in selected_seq_groups:
        if not seq_group.do_sample:
            results.append(([], []))
            continue

        seq_ids = seq_group.seq_ids
        num_parent_seqs = len(seq_ids)
        assert num_parent_seqs == 1, (
            "Greedy sampling should have only one seq.")
        parent_ids = list(range(num_parent_seqs))
        next_token_ids = [samples_lst[sample_idx]]
        results.append((next_token_ids, parent_ids))

@dataclass
class SamplingTensors:
    """Tensors for sampling."""

    temperatures: torch.Tensor
    top_ps: torch.Tensor
    top_ks: torch.Tensor
    min_ps: torch.Tensor
    presence_penalties: torch.Tensor
    frequency_penalties: torch.Tensor
    repetition_penalties: torch.Tensor
    prompt_tokens: torch.Tensor
    output_tokens: torch.Tensor

    @classmethod
    def from_sampling_metadata(
        cls,
        sampling_metadata: "SamplingMetadata",
        vocab_size: int,
        device: torch.device,
        dtype: torch.dtype,
    ) -> tuple["SamplingTensors", bool, bool, bool]:
        prompt_tokens: list[array] = []
        output_tokens: list[array] = []
        top_ks: list[int] = []
        temperatures: list[float] = []
        top_ps: list[float] = []
        min_ps: list[float] = []
        presence_penalties: list[float] = []
        frequency_penalties: list[float] = []
        repetition_penalties: list[float] = []
        do_penalties = False
        do_top_p_top_k = False
        do_min_p = False

        assert sampling_metadata.seq_groups is not None
        for seq_group in sampling_metadata.seq_groups:
            seq_ids = seq_group.seq_ids
            sampling_params = seq_group.sampling_params
            temperature = sampling_params.temperature
            p = sampling_params.presence_penalty
            f = sampling_params.frequency_penalty
            r = sampling_params.repetition_penalty
            top_p = sampling_params.top_p
            min_p = sampling_params.min_p

            # k should not be greater than the vocab size.
            top_k = min(sampling_params.top_k, vocab_size)
            top_k = vocab_size if top_k < 1 else top_k
            if temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS:
                # NOTE: Zero temperature means deterministic sampling
                # (i.e., greedy sampling or beam search).
                # Set the temperature to 1 to avoid division by zero.
                temperature = 1.0
            if not do_top_p_top_k and (top_p < 1.0 - _SAMPLING_EPS
                                       or top_k != vocab_size):
                do_top_p_top_k = True
            if not do_min_p and min_p > _SAMPLING_EPS:
                do_min_p = True
            if not do_penalties and (abs(p) >= _SAMPLING_EPS
                                     or abs(f) >= _SAMPLING_EPS
                                     or abs(r - 1.0) >= _SAMPLING_EPS):
                do_penalties = True

            is_prompt = seq_group.is_prompt
            if is_prompt and sampling_params.prompt_logprobs is not None:
                # For tokens in the prompt that we only need to get
                # their logprobs
                query_len = seq_group.query_len
                assert query_len is not None
                prefill_len = len(seq_group.prompt_logprob_indices)
                temperatures += [temperature] * prefill_len
                top_ps += [top_p] * prefill_len
                top_ks += [top_k] * prefill_len
                min_ps += [min_p] * prefill_len
                presence_penalties += [0] * prefill_len
                frequency_penalties += [0] * prefill_len
                repetition_penalties += [1] * prefill_len

            if seq_group.do_sample:
                sample_lens = len(seq_group.sample_indices)
                assert sample_lens >= len(seq_ids)
                temperatures += [temperature] * sample_lens
                top_ps += [top_p] * sample_lens
                top_ks += [top_k] * sample_lens
                min_ps += [min_p] * sample_lens
                presence_penalties += [p] * sample_lens
                frequency_penalties += [f] * sample_lens
                repetition_penalties += [r] * sample_lens

        if do_penalties:
            for seq_group in sampling_metadata.seq_groups:
                seq_ids = seq_group.seq_ids
                sampling_params = seq_group.sampling_params
                if (seq_group.is_prompt
                        and sampling_params.prompt_logprobs is not None):
                    prefill_len = len(seq_group.prompt_logprob_indices)
                    prompt_tokens.extend(
                        array(VLLM_TOKEN_ID_ARRAY_TYPE)
                        for _ in range(prefill_len))
                    output_tokens.extend(
                        array(VLLM_TOKEN_ID_ARRAY_TYPE)
                        for _ in range(prefill_len))
                if seq_group.do_sample:
                    for seq_id in seq_ids:
                        seq_data = seq_group.seq_data[seq_id]
                        prompt_tokens.append(seq_data.prompt_token_ids_array)
                        output_tokens.append(seq_data.output_token_ids_array)

        sampling_tensors = SamplingTensors.from_lists(
            temperatures,
            top_ps,
            top_ks,
            min_ps,
            presence_penalties,
            frequency_penalties,
            repetition_penalties,
            prompt_tokens,
            output_tokens,
            vocab_size,
            device,
            dtype,
        )
        return (sampling_tensors, do_penalties, do_top_p_top_k, do_min_p)

    @classmethod
    def from_lists(
        cls,

        """
        batch_update = self.batch_update_builder.get_and_reset(self.num_reqs)
        for logit_proc in self.logitsprocs.all:
            logit_proc.update_state(batch_update)

    def _make_sampling_metadata(self) -> SamplingMetadata:
        num_reqs = self.num_reqs
        if not self.all_greedy:
            temperature = copy_slice(self.temperature_cpu_tensor,
                                     self.temperature, num_reqs)
        else:
            temperature = None
        if not self.no_top_p:
            copy_slice(self.top_p_cpu_tensor, self.top_p, num_reqs)
        if not self.no_top_k:
            copy_slice(self.top_k_cpu_tensor, self.top_k, num_reqs)

        if not self.no_penalties:
            # Since syncing these tensors is expensive only copy them
            # if necessary i.e. if there are requests which require
            # penalties to be applied during sampling.
            copy_slice(self.frequency_penalties_cpu_tensor,
                       self.frequency_penalties, num_reqs)
            copy_slice(self.presence_penalties_cpu_tensor,
                       self.presence_penalties, num_reqs)
            copy_slice(self.repetition_penalties_cpu_tensor,
                       self.repetition_penalties, num_reqs)

        needs_prompt_token_ids = (not self.no_penalties or
                                  (self.num_reqs > 0
                                   and self.logits_processing_needs_token_ids))
        if needs_prompt_token_ids:
            # The prompt tokens are used only for applying penalties or
            # step pooling during the sampling/pooling process.
            # Hence copy these tensors only when there are requests which
            # need penalties/step_pooler to be applied.
            prompt_token_ids = self._make_prompt_token_ids_tensor()
        else:
            prompt_token_ids = None

        allowed_token_ids_mask: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
        if not self.no_allowed_token_ids:
            assert self.allowed_token_ids_mask is not None
            copy_slice(self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor,
                       self.allowed_token_ids_mask, num_reqs)
            allowed_token_ids_mask = self.allowed_token_ids_mask[:num_reqs]

        return SamplingMetadata(
            temperature=temperature,
            all_greedy=self.all_greedy,
            all_random=self.all_random,
            top_p=None if self.no_top_p else self.top_p[:num_reqs],
            top_k=None if self.no_top_k else self.top_k[:num_reqs],
            generators=self.generators,
            max_num_logprobs=self.max_num_logprobs,
            prompt_token_ids=prompt_token_ids,
            frequency_penalties=self.frequency_penalties[:num_reqs],
            presence_penalties=self.presence_penalties[:num_reqs],
            repetition_penalties=self.repetition_penalties[:num_reqs],
            output_token_ids=cast(list[list[int]], self.req_output_token_ids),
            no_penalties=self.no_penalties,
            allowed_token_ids_mask=allowed_token_ids_mask,
            bad_words_token_ids=self.bad_words_token_ids,
            logitsprocs=self.logitsprocs,
        )

    @property
    def pooling_metadata(self) -> PoolingMetadata:
        if len(self.pooling_params) == 0:
            pooling_params = []
        else:
            # Note, for now this assumes that all request in the batch
            # are either sampling or pooling requests
            assert len(self.req_ids) == len(self.pooling_params)
            pooling_params = [
                self.pooling_params[req_id] for req_id in self.req_ids
            ]

        return PoolingMetadata(
            prompt_lens=torch.from_numpy(
                self.num_prompt_tokens[:self.num_reqs]).to(self.device),
            prompt_token_ids=self.sampling_metadata.prompt_token_ids,
            pooling_params=pooling_params,
        )

    def _make_prompt_token_ids_tensor(self) -> torch.Tensor:
        max_prompt_len = self.num_prompt_tokens[:self.num_reqs].max()
        prompt_token_ids_cpu_tensor = torch.empty(
            (self.num_reqs, max_prompt_len),
            device="cpu",
            dtype=torch.int64,
            pin_memory=self.pin_memory,
        )
        prompt_token_ids = prompt_token_ids_cpu_tensor.numpy()
        prompt_token_ids[:] = self.token_ids_cpu[:self.
